steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the paragraph. The paragraph describes two prior research directions on convergent evolution, identifies what remains unknown between them, and then explains what motivated Price and Tan's study. The purpose must reflect how that structure works as a whole.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — To suggest that Price and Tan's conclusions would be more persuasive if they incorporated findings from the studies by Delacroix and colleagues and Nowak and colleagues.: the paragraph does not prescribe what researchers should study — it describes context for one specific study. ✗
  • Choice B — To argue that Price and Tan's study revealed a previously unrecognized issue that evolutionary biologists need to address.: the paragraph does not present a new problem or open question for readers to solve. ✗
  • Choice C — To situate Price and Tan's study in the context of their field.: the paragraph frames their work within two prior research directions and identifies the gap that motivated it — exactly what situating a study in its field means. ✓
  • Choice D — To summarize Price and Tan's criticism of the study by Delacroix and colleagues.: the paragraph does not criticize or challenge prior research — it simply maps the landscape. ✗
That is the correct answer.
